extra.message: User-controlled `username` parameter (from `sqli.get("username", "noprovided")` at line 10) is interpolated directly into an f-string SQL query passed to `cur.execute()`. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL by providing a crafted `username` value like `' OR 1=1 --`, bypassing authentication or extracting all user records.

The `INSERT INTO vulns VALUES ({vuln})` statement uses an f-string to build SQL. While the current data is hardcoded, this pattern is dangerous and would become exploitable if any `vuln` entry contained user-controlled data.

The `INSERT INTO USERS VALUES (mike, kaines)` statement uses an f-string instead of parameterized queries. Although the values are hardcoded, the pattern is unsafe and would become SQL injection if the values were user-controlled.
The `INSERT INTO USERS VALUES (admin, admin)` statement uses an f-string instead of parameterized queries. Same unsafe pattern as line 25.

User-controlled `username` parameter from `xss.get("username", "noprovided")` at line 7 is reflected directly into the response body via f-string without any escaping or sanitization. If the response content-type is interpreted as HTML (e.g., via Content-Type sniffing or Swagger UI), an attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript by providing a payload like `<script>alert(1)</script>`.

User-controlled `mathexp` parameter from `ssti.get("mathexp", "test")` at line 9 is concatenated directly into a Jinja2 `Template()` constructor. The resulting template is rendered at line 12 with `test.render()`. An attacker can achieve server-side template injection and remote code execution by providing a payload like `{{ config.__class__.__init__.__globals__['os'].popen('id').read() }}`.

The `fetchfile()` function at line 3 passes an unsanitized `name` parameter directly to `open(name).read()` (offset 30). The caller at line 13-16 uses user-controlled `filename` from `lfi.get("filename", "readme.txt")` without any path sanitization or validation. An attacker can read arbitrary files on the system (e.g., `/etc/passwd`, `../../etc/passwd`, `/proc/self/environ`).

The `fetchimage()` function at line 5 passes an unsanitized `name` parameter directly to `requests.get(url=name, timeout=2, verify=False).text` (offset 48). The caller at line 14-18 uses user-controlled `imagelink` from `rfi.get("imagelink", "http://google.com")` without any URL validation. This allows server-side request forgery (SSRF) where an attacker can make the server request internal resources, cloud metadata endpoints, or other internal services.

The `Host` header value from `request.headers["Host"]` (extracted at line 12, offset 177) is reflected directly into an HTML `<a href='{h}'>` anchor tag at line 18 without any sanitization. An attacker can inject arbitrary HTML attributes or JavaScript by providing a crafted `Host` header (e.g., `Host: attacker.com' onclick='alert(1)'`). This also enables host header injection attacks like password reset poisoning and cache poisoning.

Database exception messages are returned directly to the user via `return {"msg": f"{e}"}, 200` (line 19, offset 475). When a SQL error occurs, the full exception string (which may contain database schema information, table names, column names, or query syntax) is disclosed to the attacker. This aids in crafting further SQL injection payloads.

cwe: CWE-209: Information Exposure Through an Error Message

extra.message: Hardcoded credentials `mike:kaines` are inserted directly into the USERS table during database setup. These default credentials are visible in source code and can be used by anyone who has access to the repository.

Hardcoded credentials `admin:admin` are inserted directly into the USERS table during database setup. These default credentials are visible in source code and can be used by anyone who has access to the repository. The use of a trivial password `admin` also makes this a weak credential vulnerability.

The Flask application runs with `debug=True` on `host="0.0.0.0"`. Debug mode enables the Werkzeug debugger and interactive console, which allows arbitrary code execution when an exception occurs. Additionally, listening on `0.0.0.0` binds to all network interfaces, making the debugger accessible from any network.

The `requests.get()` call at line 5 (offset 48) uses `verify=False`, which disables SSL/TLS certificate validation. This makes the application vulnerable to man-in-the-middle (MITM) attacks where an attacker can intercept and modify the HTTPS response, or serve malicious content under a spoofed certificate.

SQL query results (including user credentials such as username and password hashes if applicable) are returned directly to the client at line 20 via `return {"msg": f"Hello, {users}"}, 200`. The entire `users` tuple/list is serialized into the response, exposing all stored user data including the password column.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
